commit.message:
Add kylec-rpf to the CLA bot allowlist (#726)

## Status

- N/A

## Points for consideration:

- Security: updates the contributor allowlist only; no auth or runtime
behavior changes
- Performance: none

## What's changed?

Adds `kylec-rpf` to `.clabot` and preserves the existing alphabetical
ordering of the contributor allowlist.

- **CLA bot configuration**
  - Added `kylec-rpf` to the `contributors` array
- Kept the list sorted to match the file’s existing maintenance
convention

```json
{
  "contributors": [
    "jrmhaig",
    "KristinaDudnyk",
    "kylec-rpf",
    "loiswells97"
  ]
}
```

## Steps to perform after deploying to production

None.

Co-authored-by: teetee971 <95961170+teetee971@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor: Extract theme resolution helper to eliminate code duplication

Extract duplicated theme resolution logic into a shared prepareDrawContext
helper function. This eliminates 12 lines of duplicated code across Button,
TextInput, Label, and ScrollView Draw methods.

Changes:
- Add prepareDrawContext() helper to resolve position, bounds, and theme
- Add drawContext struct to hold resolved draw parameters
- Refactor Button.Draw to use prepareDrawContext
- Refactor TextInput.Draw to use prepareDrawContext
- Refactor Label.Draw to use prepareDrawContext
- Refactor ScrollView.Draw to use prepareDrawContext

Impact:
- Duplication ratio: 0.32% → 0% (eliminated 12 duplicate lines)
- Code quality score: 100/100 (maintained)
- No behavioral changes
- All tests pass, go vet clean

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enable screen saver and keep-display-on by default (#45)

* Enable screen saver and keep-display-on by default

Most users will want the display to stay on (since the HAP server
can't run in the background) with the screen saver active. Change
both defaults to true and only override from UserDefaults when a
saved value exists, so existing users who explicitly disabled them
keep their preference. Rename the card title to "Keep Display On"
for clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update Pylo/HAPViewModel.swift

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Idempotent annotation to all read-only MCP tools

MCP best practices recommend safety annotations (readOnlyHint,
destructiveHint, idempotent) on all tools. Our read-only tools
were missing Idempotent = true, which signals to clients that
these tools are safe to retry and cache.

commit.message:
android: fix 'Settings is not a type' QML crash

Add 'import QtCore' to android/qml/SettingsTab.qml.
The Settings QML type requires this import in Qt 6 — without it
QQmlApplicationEngine fails to load, calls exit(-1), and HWUI
crashes on mutex destruction (SIGABRT in hwuiTask).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

proceeding.

Apply suggestion from @Copilot

remove unnecessary cd.detectChanges()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix tokio runtime starvation from synchronous sled flush during sync

- Skip per-block storage flush when is_syncing is true (save_block,
  save_block_without_height_update, update_chain_height)
- Flush at batch boundaries in handle_blocks_response and sync_from_peers
- Final flush before clearing is_syncing flag
- Yield every 10 blocks in handle_blocks_response to let RPC/timers run
- Cap scan_forward loop to 100 iterations to prevent long blocking
- Add startup separator in logs for daemon restart visibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Initialer Projektentwurf - Sonnenuhr Wallpaper-Generator (.NET 10)

IHK-Abschlussprojekt: Entwicklung einer C#-Desktop-Anwendung zur automatisierten
Generierung standortspezifischer Sonnenuhr-Wallpaper fuer Windows 11.

Struktur (.NET 10 / Windows Forms):
- Models: Location, SolarData, WallpaperConfig, AppSettings
- Services: SolarApiService, SundialCalculator, WallpaperGeneratorService,
  WallpaperRegistryService, ConfigurationService
- Forms: MainForm, ConfigurationForm, System-Tray-Integration
- Tests (xUnit): 24 Tests bestanden

Code-Qualitaet:
- EVA-Prinzip in allen Methoden (Eingabe-Verarbeitung-Ausgabe)
- XML-Dokumentationskommentare auf allen public Members
- Nullable Reference Types, Async/Await, IDisposable-Pattern

Dokumentation (IHK):
- Pflichtenheft, Klassendiagramm (Mermaid), Aktivitaetsdiagramm (Mermaid)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(api): move webhooks to unified /api/webhooks/{service} path

Move Paddle and Sentry webhook handlers out of versioned API routes
into a dedicated /api/webhooks/ directory. Webhooks are inbound event
receivers from external services with their own auth (HMAC signatures),
not part of the versioned API contract consumed by frontends.

Old paths:
  - POST /api/v1/payments/paddle/webhook
  - POST /api/v1/webhooks/sentry

New paths:
  - POST /api/webhooks/paddle
  - POST /api/webhooks/sentry

All 14 Paddle processor files moved with the handler.
Updated API docs and README to reflect new paths.

BREAKING: Paddle dashboard webhook URL must be updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add README

commit.message:
Add daily-data MCP server

Standalone MCP server that exposes Daily app data (tasks, timeblocks,
team, reviews) as 19 tools via the Model Context Protocol. Uses stdio
transport for integration with Copilot CLI.

Tools: CRUD for tasks, timeblocks, team, reviews + data_path utility.
Reads/writes the same JSON files as the Electron app.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix missing static web assets in NuGet package (#118)

The Razor SDK generates the static web assets manifest at dotnet build
time. Previously, npm run build (which outputs the JS files to wwwroot/)
ran after dotnet build, so the manifest was generated with an empty
wwwroot/ and dotnet pack --no-build reused that stale manifest.

Fix by moving the npm install/lint/build steps before dotnet restore and
dotnet build, ensuring the compiled JS files exist in wwwroot/ when the
static web assets manifest is generated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: D1非対応のdb.transaction()をRETURNING付きUPDATEに置き換え

Cloudflare D1はBEGIN/COMMITを使うトランザクションをサポートしないため
db.transaction()を削除。answeredAt IS NULL AND userId一致の条件付きUPDATEは
SQLiteレベルでアトミックなため、重複書き込み防止の効果は同等。

commit.message:
feat(ol-superset): add apply-rls command for governance RLS policy management (#1971)

* feat(ol-superset): add apply-rls command for governance RLS policy management

Add a new `apply-rls` CLI command that applies OL governance row-level
security (RLS) policies to a Superset instance using OAuth PKCE
authentication (no shared admin credentials required).

New files:
- ol_superset/commands/apply_rls.py: cyclopts command with dry-run
  support, production confirmation prompt, and rich table output
- policies/ol_rls_policies.json: canonical RLS filter definitions
  (moved from ol-infrastructure; now co-located with the CLI)

Updated files:
- lib/superset_api.py: four new RLS API helpers (list_roles,
  list_datasets_name_to_id, list_rls_filters, apply_rls_filter) with
  collision detection for ambiguous bare table names
- cli.py: register apply-rls command and add workflow example

This replaces the K8s Job approach (which required an admin password
stored in Vault that was never populated) with an operator-driven
workflow that uses each user's own Keycloak credentials.

Usage:
  ol-superset apply-rls superset-production      # with confirmation
  ol-superset apply-rls superset-qa --yes        # skip prompt
  ol-superset apply-rls superset-production --dry-run

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(wizard): use power-law model for Est Time column

The xatlas UV unwrap estimate used a linear model (1 min per 100k faces)
while the pipeline itself uses a superlinear power-law (k * n^1.79).
This caused the wizard Est Time to be lower than the actual xatlas step.
Align both to the same power-law coefficients.

Also includes user README edits.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
